summary: Stunning soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1978 performance, highlighted by scorching Sugaree, Tennessee Jed and an explosive Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World.
review: The show bursts with raw energy – Jerry is on fire, Phil and Bob lock in tight, and the band delivers blistering versions of Sugaree, Tennessee Jed, Estimated Prophet and Eyes of the World. The soundboard source is crystal‑clear, offering remarkable detail on the interplay between guitar and rhythm while only a few keyboard moments are slightly harsh. Minor criticism centers on an overly rushed Eyes of the World, but overall it’s a masterful listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Both the musical performance and recording quality are rated highly by reviewers; only one comment notes a rushed song, resulting in an overall strong but not flawless rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Exceptional clarity and detail, especially on Jerry‑Bob interplay; a few strident keyboard spots from Keith but they have minimal impact. Overall crisp, balanced mix with great live feel.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set launches with classic Dead goodness and high‑octane energy, maintaining momentum through fiery mid‑set jams and culminating in a soaring Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence; the show feels like a nonstop rocket from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: In fine form, tearing it up with an unforgettable intro into the Me & My Uncle jam; tone described as perfect.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lock‑step with Jerry, adding his own distinct flavor; part of the “effervescent voodoo” interplay praised.
band_member_comments.Bob: Locks in tightly with Jerry, delivering percussive funk licks and screaming bent‑note explosions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s playing is occasionally strident and misses the groove but has a de minimis effect on the overall masterpiece.
band_member_comments.Drums: Compelling throughout; drums and space sections are highlighted as engaging.
